scope_summary: Establishes the WAPC's requirements for subdividing land into residential lots throughout Western Australia, operating alongside the R-Codes Volume 1 minimum/average lot size and frontage provisions (Table D). It sets criteria for general lot suitability, lot size variations (capped at five per cent in defined circumstances), single residential lots, battleaxe subdivision, vehicle access, screen fencing and utility servicing (sewerage and underground power). It is an Operational Policy made under s 14(b)(ii) of the Planning and Development Act 2005 and is the current version of the policy formerly known as DC 2.2.

key_numeric_standards.topic: maximum minimum/average lot size variation (one lot only, criteria apply)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: residential subdivision in existing residential areas
battleaxe lots coded R50 and higher

key_numeric_standards.topic: constructed driveway width within battleaxe leg
minimum effective lot area for battleaxe lots outside R-Codes areas
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: residential battleaxe lots in locations not subject to the R-Codes

key_numeric_standards.topic: truncation where access leg joins effective lot area
truncation where access leg meets street reserve
maximum number of battleaxe access legs in one arrangement (absent exceptional circumstances)

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: uniform fencing where lots abut public reserves

key_numeric_standards.topic: screen fence maximum height (visually permeable above 1.2 m)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: uniform fencing where lots abut public reserves

definitions: Battleaxe lot (as defined in the R-Codes)
Effective lot area (as defined in the R-Codes)
